input.headline: The World in Pictures Get The AP’s most compelling photographs sent directly to your inbox.

raw.headline: The World in Pictures Get The AP’s most compelling photographs sent directly to your inbox.

winner: I always thought my inbox was a magical window to another world, until I started receiving physical photos instead.

candidates.text: I always thought my inbox was a magical window to another world, until I started receiving physical photos instead.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on the idea of an inbox, specific to receiving physical photos, concise and naturally funny."}

candidates.text: Why did the photo go to school? To get a better education in composition and lighting!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat creative but relies on a common pun ('education' as both learning and photographic technique). It's brief and specific to the topic, but lacks surprising depth or originality."}

candidates.text: Well, I've always found that a world full of pictures can sometimes be a little heavy on the weight, but light on the reading.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat clever with wordplay on 'heavy' and 'light,' but it's a bit vague and lacks the punch needed for maximum humor. It also doesn't fully engage with the headline's promise of compelling photographs."}

candidates.text: Why can't photos send emails? They don't have thumbs!
Why did the photo refuse to go to school? Because it didn't want to leave its inbox.
